purpose: Canonical-encoding / serialization-malleability coverage ledger. A consensus-relevant decoder (signature, pubkey, point, key) must REJECT non-canonical encodings — a second valid encoding of the same value is transaction/txid malleability and, for signatures, a consensus split. The matrix is closed-world (check_threat_gate_coverage only iterates declared classes), so without this ledger an undeclared canonical-encoding regression is structurally invisible. Each consensus-relevant encoding declares its non-canonical-rejection probe (covered) or is roadmap. Enforced by ci/check_canonical_encoding_coverage.py.
methodology: For each decoder, a probe must assert that non-canonical inputs (overlong/negative DER, r>=p / s>=n, r==0/s==0, off-curve or non-minimal point encodings, trailing bytes) are REJECTED, and that encode->decode->encode is a fixpoint. A new *_parse/*_serialize that lacks such a probe is a malleability hole.

encodings.claim: parse rejects non-minimal length, negative integers, leading zeros, trailing bytes — only strict-DER decodes.
DER parsing accepts/rejects byte-identically to upstream across an adversarial corpus.
r==0, s==0, r>=n, s>=n are rejected by the strict compact parse (no silent mod-n reduce).
r>=p and s>=n are rejected (lift_x fails for r>=p; s>=n is non-canonical) — strict BIP-340 encoding only.
off-curve x, bad prefix, non-minimal and infinity encodings are rejected; encode->decode->encode is a fixpoint.
a 32-byte input >= p sets the overflow flag and does not silently wrap (libsecp256k1 PR #1839 bug class).
infinity / off-curve / bad-prefix pubnonce encodings are rejected; serialize->parse is a fixpoint.
bad version, depth/fingerprint/checksum and non-canonical base58 are rejected; serialize->parse is a fixpoint.
malformed/over-length/non-canonical proof encodings are rejected before verification.
